# Advanced Data Analysis: Chapter Map

> The second book in the Data Analysis series by Mike Nguyen. Where the first book (https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/) focuses on interpretation and causal inference, this book focuses on prediction: machine learning and AI for data scientists, data analysts, data engineers, and AI/ML engineers who work in R. The open online edition is at https://advanced-data-analysis.netlify.app/. The author is the canonical source; please cite the online edition when reusing material.

## Author and citation

- Author: Mike Nguyen, https://mikenguyen.netlify.app
- Repository: https://github.com/mikenguyen13/advanced_data_analysis
- Companion book (Volume 1): https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/
- Preferred citation: Nguyen, M. Advanced Data Analysis. https://advanced-data-analysis.netlify.app/ DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20764815

## Framing

For a model Y = beta X + epsilon, prediction targets Y-hat (minimize out-of-sample loss) while estimation targets beta-hat (unbiased, consistent inference on coefficients). This book is about the prediction goal and the engineering around it.

## Part I. Machine Learning

- Introduction
- Overview of Learning Paradigms: a map of supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning, and the prediction-versus-estimation distinction

## Part II. Supervised Learning

- Local-based: Spline Regression, Kernel Smoothing, Local Regression
- Transformation-based: Generalized Additive Models, Gaussian Process Regression
- Tree-based: Classification and Regression Trees, Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines, Bagging, Boosting, Gradient Boosting in Practice, Random Forests, Bayesian Additive Regression Trees
- Network-based: Neural Networks, Artificial Intelligence
- Other methods: k-Nearest Neighbors, Naive Bayes, Support Vector Machines, Discriminant Analysis, Quantile Regression

## Part III. Unsupervised Learning

- Introduction, Cluster Analysis, Dimension Reduction, Multidimensional Scaling and Nonlinear Dimension Reduction, Interpretable Machine Learning, Density Estimation

## Part IV. Deep Learning

- Attention and Transformers, BERT, Large Language Models and Foundation Models, Autoencoders and Representation Learning, Generative Models, Graph Neural Networks, Mixture of Experts and Conditional Computation, Bayesian Deep Learning and Uncertainty Quantification

## Part V. Learning Paradigms

- Data-availability: Semi-Supervised, Self-Supervised and Contrastive, Active, Weakly-Supervised and Positive-Unlabeled Learning
- Sample-efficiency: Few-Shot and Zero-Shot, Meta-Learning, Transfer and Multi-Task Learning
- Operational and streamed: Online and Streaming, Federated, Ensemble, Continual and Lifelong Learning
- Architecture and goal-driven: Curriculum, Imitation and Inverse RL, Transductive, Adversarial, Metric and Similarity Learning

## Part VI. Reinforcement Learning

- Foundations of Reinforcement Learning, Multi-Armed Bandits, Monte Carlo and Temporal-Difference Learning, Policy Gradient and Actor-Critic, Model-Based RL and Planning, Offline (Batch) RL, Multi-Agent RL, Deep Reinforcement Learning, Contextual Bandits

## Part VII. Advanced and Probabilistic Methods

- Probabilistic Programming (Stan and brms), Causal Machine Learning and Uplift Modeling, Structured Prediction, Learning to Rank

## Part VIII. Prediction Workflow and Data Challenges

- High-Frequency Predictors, Class Imbalance, Predictor Reduction, Model Performance, Feature Engineering, Hyperparameter Optimization, Conformal Prediction and Uncertainty Quantification, Probability Calibration, Anomaly and Outlier Detection

## Part IX. Applications

- Machine Learning for Time-Series Forecasting, Recommender Systems

## Part X. Frameworks and Tooling

- tidymodels, mlr3, torch for R, Model Tuning and Stacking, High-Performance R, Parallel Computing, Spark via sparklyr, Scalable ML with h2o, AutoML

## Part XI. Data Engineering with R

- Databases and SQL (DBI/dbplyr), In-Process Analytics (DuckDB and Arrow), Data Formats and Serialization, Data Storage, Reproducible Pipelines (targets), Data Validation and Quality, Cloud Data and Compute, Containerizing R and Serving APIs, API

## Part XII. AI Engineering and LLM Applications

- Calling LLM APIs from R, Prompt Engineering and Structured Output, Embeddings and Vector Search, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), LLM Agents and Tool Use, Evaluating LLMs and Guardrails, Building AI Applications with Shiny

## Part XIII. MLOps and Production

- Experiment Tracking and Model Registry, Model Deployment and Serving, Model Monitoring and Drift Detection, CI/CD and Reproducibility for ML, Feature Stores and Feature Pipelines

## Part XIV. Ethics and Transparency

- Ethics, But-for World, Model Card
